Goal setting.title: Lesson 1: What Makes an Effective Goal?
objectives: Understand the difference between vague and specific goals
Identify why goals help motivation and wellbeing
Goal setting.activityPrompt: Write one vague goal you have, then rewrite it as a SMART goal.

quiz.question: Which of the following is a SMART goal?
options: I want to get better at school work
I will improve my English grade from a 5 to a 6 by revising twice a week
I hope to be fitter someday
I want to be more confident

Goal setting.title: Lesson 2: Breaking Goals Into Steps
objectives: Understand why large goals need smaller actions
Learn how to plan steps in order
Create an action plan for a personal goal
Goal setting.activityPrompt: Choose a goal and break it into 3–5 small, achievable steps.

quiz.question: Why do we break goals into smaller steps?
options: To make them look more impressive
So we can do everything at the last minute
To make progress feel manageable and achievable
To avoid doing any hard work

Goal setting.title: Lesson 3: Motivation and Barriers
objectives: Recognise common barriers that can stop progress
Goal setting.activityPrompt: List one goal and name 2 barriers + 2 strategies for overcoming them.

quiz.question: Which is an example of a motivation strategy?
options: Ignoring obstacles and hoping they go away
Setting rewards for completing steps
Doing everything without breaks
Waiting until motivation appears

Goal setting.title: Lesson 4: Tracking Progress
objectives: Understand why progress tracking improves success
Learn simple tracking methods (journals, checklists, apps)
Reflect on progress to adjust goals
Goal setting.activityPrompt: Create a simple progress tracker (table, checklist or calendar).

quiz.question: Why is tracking progress important?
options: It pressures you to be perfect
It helps you notice improvement and adjust plans
It guarantees automatic success
It replaces the need for effort

Goal setting.title: Lesson 5: Reviewing and Resetting Goals
objectives: Understand why goals sometimes need changing
Learn how to evaluate success honestly
Reflect on what worked and what didn’t
Goal setting.activityPrompt: Reflect on a past goal. What worked? What would you change next time?

quiz.question: When should you reset a goal?
options: When it no longer fits your needs or circumstances
Whenever you feel bored
As soon as someone else sets a goal
You should never change goals once set
